api.label: API
api.title: API Access
api.aiContextDescriptionNoToken: Copy this documentation to your AI assistant (ChatGPT, Claude, etc.). Click 'Generate Token' to create an API token that will be automatically filled in.
api.aiContextDescriptionWithToken: Copy this documentation to your AI assistant (ChatGPT, Claude, etc.). A permission-restricted API token has been generated and filled in.
api.confirmDescription: This will create a new API token with the following permissions for this base:
api.confirmExpiry: The token will be valid for 1 year.
api.tokenCreatedSuccess: Token created successfully! Click copy to use it with your AI assistant.
api.advancedDesc: Open the advanced API query builder for more customization options.
api.queryBuilderDesc: Visually configure filters, sorting, and other parameters to generate ready-to-run API request code.

personalView.tip: After enabling, the view settings will only take effect for you personally

dialog.title: Exit personal mode
dialog.description: The personal view configuration will be restored to the real-time collaboration state, you can also save the personal view settings and synchronize them to everyone

welcome.title: Welcome
welcome.description: Click the "+" button in the sidebar to add resources

link.batch_duplicate: Cannot link record(s): already linked by another record in the same batch. In one-to-many relationships, each record can only belong to one parent.
link.one_many_duplicate: Cannot link record(s): already linked to another record. In one-to-many relationships, each record can only belong to one parent.
link.one_one_duplicate: Cannot link record(s): the target record is already linked by another record in a one-to-one relationship.

field.maxColumnLimit: Table "{{tableName}}" can have at most {{maxFieldCount}} fields.

field.fieldManagementDesc: Detailed properties for all fields of the current table

funcDesc.and: Returns true if all the values are true
funcDesc.arrayCompact: Removes empty strings and null values from the array. Keeps 'false' and strings that contain one or more blank characters.
funcDesc.arrayJoin: Join all the values into a single comma-separated string.
funcDesc.concatenate: Joins together the text values into a single text value.
funcDesc.count: Count only non-empty numeric values. If you want to count all records, use COUNTALL.
funcDesc.countA: Count the number of non-empty values. This function counts both numeric and text values.
funcDesc.countAll: counts all values including blank records.
funcDesc.max: Returns the largest of the given numbers.
funcDesc.min: Returns the smallest of the given numbers.
funcDesc.or: Returns true if any one of the values is true.
funcDesc.xor: Returns true if and only if odd number of values are true.

editor.allowLinkToDuplicateRecords: Allows records to be selected more than once

editor.doSaveChanges: Do you want to save the changes you made?
editor.linkFieldToLookup: Linked record field to use for lookup

conditionalLookup.limitPlaceholder: Leave blank to include all matches

conditionalLookup.sortMissingWarningDescription: The field that powered this sort was deleted. Results ignore the sort and only enforce the limit.

editor.noLinkTip: No linked records to look up. Add a Link to another record field, then try to configure your lookup again.

subTitle.link: Link to records in the table you choose
subTitle.singleLineText: Enter text, or prefill each new cell with a default value.
subTitle.attachment: Add or AI generate images, or upload documents or other files to be viewed or downloaded.
subTitle.checkbox: Check or uncheck to indicate status.
subTitle.multipleSelect: Select one or more predefined options in a list.
subTitle.singleSelect: Select one predefined option from a list, or prefill each new cell with a default option.
subTitle.user: Add an user to a record.
subTitle.date: Enter a date (e.g. 11/12/2023) or choose one from a calendar.
subTitle.number: Enter a number, or prefill each new cell with a default value.
subTitle.duration: Enter a duration of time in hours, minutes or seconds (e.g. 1:23).
subTitle.rating: Add a rating on a predefined scale.
subTitle.conditionalRollup: Summarize values from a linked table with conditional filters.
subTitle.conditionalLookup: Show linked values that meet your filter conditions.
subTitle.count: Count the number of linked records.
subTitle.createdTime: See the date and time each record was created.
subTitle.lastModifiedTime: See the date and time of the most recent edit to some or all fields in a record.
subTitle.createdBy: See which user created the record.
subTitle.lastModifiedBy: See which user made the most recent edit to some or all fields in a record.
subTitle.autoNumber: Automatically generate unique incremental numbers for each record.
subTitle.lookup: See values from a field in a linked record.

placeholder.translate: Translation concise and easy to understand, light-hearted

v2.dialogDescription: Run the v2 schema checker and repair flow for {{tableName}}.
v2.dialogDescriptionNoTable: Run the v2 schema checker and repair flow for the current base.
v2.repairUnavailable: This rule cannot be repaired automatically
v2.manualRepairNoticeWithCount: {{count}} issues need manual repair. Use the "Manual" action on the right side of each rule to open the repair dialog.
v2.manualRepairDialogDescription: This issue cannot be repaired automatically. Review the guidance below and choose how it should be fixed.
v2.manualRepairDialogHint: This dialog currently guides the user through the repair choices required by the rule. Execution can be added on top of this contract later.
v2.manualRepairPreviewTip: Manual repair is not submitted yet. This preview shows the options the rule asks the user to choose from.
v2.repairPreviewDescription: These details come from the repair dry run. The repair will only execute after you confirm.
v2.repairPreviewTooltip: Open the dry-run repair plan. The dialog will show the returned SQL, or clearly say when no SQL is available.
v2.repairPreviewMissingTable: This check result does not include a table, so the dry-run repair target cannot be resolved.
v2.repairPreviewUnavailableStatus: This status does not need repair, so a dry-run repair plan cannot be generated.
v2.repairPreviewNoPrinciple: This rule did not return additional repair guidance.
v2.repairPreviewNoSql: The dry run did not return executable SQL, so automatic repair cannot be run directly.
v2.repairPreviewCannotConfirm: The dry run returned SQL, but this rule cannot be confirmed for automatic repair right now. You can still review the repair plan.
v2.streamError: Failed to stream schema integrity results.
v2.noTableSelected: Select a table first to run the v2 schema checker.
v2.noFilteredResults: No results match the current filters.

message.skippedDependencies: Skipped because dependencies are not satisfied
message.baseCheckStreamConnected: Base schema integrity check stream connected
message.baseRepairStreamConnected: Base schema integrity repair stream connected
message.skippedStatusNotSelected: Skipped because this status level was not selected for repair
message.skippedRepairUnavailable: Skipped because automatic repair is unavailable in the current state

detail.systemColumnNotNull: System column "{{columnName}}" should be NOT NULL
detail.systemColumnUnique: System column "{{columnName}}" should have a UNIQUE index
detail.systemColumnPrimaryKey: System column "{{columnName}}" should be PRIMARY KEY
detail.systemColumnDefault: System column "{{columnName}}" should have a default expression
detail.columnUniqueMissingDescription: Add a UNIQUE constraint to "{{fieldName}}" so duplicate values cannot be written.
detail.columnUniqueIndexMismatch: Field "{{fieldName}}" is missing the expected unique index
detail.columnUniqueIndexMismatchDescription: Rebuild the index for "{{fieldName}}" as a unique index so reads and writes follow the field's uniqueness rule.
detail.foreignKeyMissing: Field "{{fieldName}}" is missing its foreign key constraint
detail.foreignKeyMissingDescription: Restore the foreign key from "{{fieldName}}" to "{{targetTableName}}" so linked records stay consistent.
detail.foreignKeyTargetTableMissing: The linked table for "{{fieldName}}" cannot be found
detail.foreignKeyTargetTableMissingDescription: Automatic repair cannot restore the foreign key for "{{fieldName}}" because the target table "{{targetPhysicalTableName}}" does not exist.
detail.foreignKeyOrphanRows: Field "{{fieldName}}" still has {{count}} rows pointing to missing linked records
detail.foreignKeyOrphanRowsDescription: Automatic repair cannot restore the foreign key for "{{fieldName}}" until the {{count}} invalid references to "{{targetTableName}}" are cleaned up.
detail.junctionForeignKeyMissing: The junction table for "{{fieldName}}" is missing one of its foreign keys
detail.junctionForeignKeyMissingDescription: Restore the junction foreign key for "{{fieldName}}" so relation rows stay linked to existing records in "{{targetTableName}}".
detail.junctionForeignKeyTargetTableMissing: The linked table for the junction of "{{fieldName}}" cannot be found
detail.junctionForeignKeyTargetTableMissingDesc: Automatic repair cannot restore the junction foreign key for "{{fieldName}}" because the target table "{{targetPhysicalTableName}}" does not exist.
detail.junctionForeignKeyOrphanRows: The junction table for "{{fieldName}}" still has {{count}} rows pointing to missing linked records
detail.junctionForeignKeyOrphanRowsDescription: Automatic repair cannot restore the junction foreign key for "{{fieldName}}" until the {{count}} invalid relation rows referencing "{{targetTableName}}" are cleaned up.
detail.referenceMissing: Field "{{fieldName}}" is missing a reference mapping
detail.referenceMissingDescription: Restore the reference metadata between "{{fieldName}}" and its dependency field so lookup and relation updates can propagate correctly.
detail.symmetricFieldTargetMissing: The paired link field for "{{fieldName}}" cannot be found
detail.symmetricFieldWrongType: The paired field for "{{fieldName}}" is not a link field
detail.symmetricFieldInvalidOptions: The paired field for "{{fieldName}}" has invalid link configuration
detail.symmetricFieldMissingBackReference: The paired field for "{{fieldName}}" does not point back to the current field
detail.symmetricFieldWrongBackReference: The paired field for "{{fieldName}}" points to another field instead of the current one
detail.symmetricFieldDuplicateUsage: The symmetric link target "{{symmetricFieldId}}" is reused by "{{conflictFieldName}}"
detail.symmetricFieldDuplicateUsageDescription: Each two-way link pair must have a unique symmetric field. Choose which field should keep this relationship before repairing.

reason.manualRule: This rule requires a manual decision
reason.statementGenerationFailed: The system could not generate a safe repair statement
reason.noStatements: This rule has no automatic repair statement
reason.symmetricFieldConflict: This symmetric link conflict needs a manual choice
reason.foreignKeyTargetTableMissing: Automatic repair is unavailable because the linked table is missing
reason.foreignKeyOrphanRows: Automatic repair is unavailable because invalid linked rows still exist
reason.junctionForeignKeyTargetTableMissing: Automatic repair is unavailable because the junction target table is missing
reason.junctionForeignKeyOrphanRows: Automatic repair is unavailable because invalid junction rows still exist

description.autoRule: The repair will execute the schema statements generated by this rule, then re-check the rule to confirm the schema is valid.
description.symmetricFieldConflict: More than one field points at the same symmetric link target. Pick which field should own the two-way relationship before repairing.
description.foreignKeyTargetTableMissing: Check whether the linked table {{targetPhysicalTableName}} was deleted or renamed. Recreate the table, or update/remove the link configuration for "{{fieldName}}", then run the check again.
description.foreignKeyOrphanRows: Clean up the invalid linked rows for "{{fieldName}}" before adding the foreign key constraint again.
description.junctionForeignKeyTargetTableMissing: Check whether the target table {{targetPhysicalTableName}} was deleted or renamed. Recreate the table, or update/remove the link configuration for "{{fieldName}}", then run the check again.
description.junctionForeignKeyOrphanRows: Clean up the invalid junction rows for "{{fieldName}}" before adding the foreign key back.

symmetricField.title: Resolve symmetric link conflict
symmetricField.description: Pick how this duplicate symmetric link should be repaired.
symmetricField.resolutionDescription: Choose which field should keep the two-way link behavior.

option.keepCurrent: Keep the current field as the two-way link
option.keepDuplicate: Keep the duplicate field as the two-way link
option.convertDuplicate: Convert the duplicate field to a one-way link

index.description: Indexes can significantly improve search performance, especially when dealing with large amounts of data (over {{rowCount}} rows). The downside is slightly slower write operations. If you frequently perform searches or have large datasets, it's recommended to enable indexing.
index.repairTip: Index anomalies detected, which may cause search performance degradation. It is recommended to click the repair button to fix the index
index.enableIndexTip: You are creating an index. The time required depends on the table size. During this process, the table's read and write performance may be affected. Please be patient.
index.globalSearchTip_limited: Search all fields except date, checkbox and button fields, maximum {{count}} fields
index.globalSearchTip_infinity: Search all fields except date, checkbox and button fields
index.autoIndexTip: Your table has exceeded {{rowCount}} rows. We recommend enabling indexing to improve search performance. Please note that indexing may slightly reduce write speed. During index creation, table read/write performance might be temporarily affected. Please be patient.

searchTips.maxFieldTips_limited: Exceeded the maximum {{count}} number of fields, extra fields will be ignored

tips.importWayTip: Click or drag file to this area to upload
tips.leaveTip: Your data will still be imported.
tips.fileExceedSizeTip: This type file size exceeds the limit of
tips.importAlert: Once the import begins, it cannot be stopped until it is either successfully completed or terminated due to failure. The import status is displayed in the upper right corner of the table. The results of import process will be notified to you once completed. You should not update the field during the import as it may cause errors.
